profile_diff_candidates.proposed_change: Add a standing analytical lens for AI tooling vendor due diligence in regulated environments: when evaluating AI-assisted development or productivity tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot), proactively surface compliance posture (BAA availability, FedRAMP status, data routing architecture, audit trail capabilities) alongside capability assessment. For an SEC-registered RIA, this means flagging cloud-only architectures with no BAA as presumptively non-compliant without additional controls, not as a caveat but as a leading finding.
profile_diff_candidates.rationale: The Cursor/compliance post directly maps to the user's regulated environment context and reflects a real due diligence pattern — evaluating AI tooling against HIPAA/SOC2/audit trail requirements. Multiple comments reference Claude Code as a near-term evaluation candidate, making this directly relevant. The user's profile already covers vendor due diligence but this adds specificity to the AI tooling category, which is increasingly a board-level governance question in RIA environments.

behavior_watch.pattern: Multiple posts praise Claude's ability to build and render functional applications end-to-end (React apps, fantasy sports platforms, games) with minimal iteration, suggesting strong vibe-coding and rapid prototyping capability.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: All supporting posts describe developer/hobbyist use cases; the user's profile explicitly excludes developer-persona use cases, and no posts address executive or governance tooling applications of this capability.

behavior_watch.pattern: Posts from 2023 praising Claude as superior to ChatGPT on nuance, depth, and academic topic handling represent early-era comparative signal that has since been superseded by model generations.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: Signal is too dated (Claude 1/2 era) to support actionable configuration changes; no pattern in this batch meets the 3-post threshold for current, relevant behavior.

behavior_watch.pattern: Claude.ai reliability and uptime are recurring community concerns, with multiple outage events generating significant signal about API 500 errors and service degradation affecting workflow-dependent users.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: This reflects platform infrastructure issues outside configuration scope — no memory edit or profile change can mitigate upstream availability failures, and the user's profile does not currently address fallback workflow protocols.

behavior_watch.pattern: Community discussion surfaces a fan-out prompting pattern for long-form document production — generating an outline, then using parallel completions per section — as a potential quality improvement over single-pass generation.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: Only one post addresses this pattern directly, falling below the 3-post evidence threshold; flagging for future signal accumulation given the user's heavy document production use case.

behavior_watch.pattern: Claude capacity throttling and rate limits are causing mid-session interruptions that disrupt sustained analytical work sessions.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: This is a platform infrastructure constraint, not a configuration or behavior issue — no prompt engineering or memory edit can address session limit exhaustion.

behavior_watch.pattern: Users report Claude's over-refusal on edge-case prompts as a persistent friction point, though Anthropic claims improvement with Claude 3.
behavior_watch.why_not_actioned: Only 2 posts in this batch surface this pattern with sufficient specificity; does not meet the 3-post evidence threshold for candidate generation.
